subject: Ensure sdnctl user/schema created on master MySQL
commit_message:
Ensure sdnctl user/schema created on master MySQL

Change-Id: I5507d23ff62c9d5d5d19f86374ea91ccea2e95a9
Signed-off-by: jmac <james.macnider@amdocs.com>
Issue-ID: OOM-887

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Similar changes would also be required for APP-C if/when they decide to deploy more than one MySQL node in the future.

Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Something still not right.  I had to disable persistence and the nfs provisioner because I run a k8s cluster.
I did so like this:
sdnc:
  enabled: true
  mysql:
    disableNfsProvisioner: true
    persistence:
      enabled: false

Things seem like the come up but the sdnc app pod is in a restart loop.  I suspect the liveness probe is restarting it because port 8181 isn't open? Here are some logs.

02:29:54.265 [main] INFO  o.o.c.sli.core.sli.SvcLogicLoader - Activating SvcLogicGraph [module=SUBNET-API, rpc=subnet-allocated-notification, mode=sync, version=1.3.1-SNAPSHOT]
Load and activate finished at Wed Apr 11 02:29:54 UTC 2018
Load and activate took 3.82862663269 seconds
Installing Opendaylight cluster features
Authentication failed
Authentication failed
Update cluster information statically
Get current Hostname deep-sdnc-0
################################################
##             Configure Cluster              ##
################################################
Configuring unique name in akka.conf
Configuring hostname in akka.conf
Configuring data and rpc seed nodes in akka.conf
modules = [
	
	{
		name = "inventory"
		namespace = "urn:opendaylight:inventory"
		shard-strategy = "module"
	},
	{
		name = "topology"
		namespace = "urn:TBD:params:xml:ns:yang:network-topology"
		shard-strategy = "module"
	},
	{
		name = "toaster"
		namespace = "http://netconfcentral.org/ns/toaster"
		shard-strategy = "module"
	}
]
Configuring replication type in module-shards.conf
################################################
##   NOTE: Manually restart controller to     ##
##         apply configuration.               ##
################################################
Restarting OpenDaylight
karaf: There is a Root instance already running with name root and pid 240




Also the sdnc portal is restarting with these logs:

Warning: connect.session() MemoryStore is not
designed for a production environment, as it will leak
memory, and will not scale past a single process.
index.js
dbFabric=false
checking dbIP:null
Express server listening on port 8843
Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:3306

I am not sure if this patch is meant to result in the SDNC staying running or if it fixes something else that I don't know what to look for.

Patch Set 1:

This can't be verified currently because the SDN-C container itself changed and isn't stable.  When this patch was posted, everything came up cleanly.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2 Verified+1

NAME                                        READY     STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
deep-sdnc-0                                 2/2       Running   0          27m
deep-sdnc-db-0                              2/2       Running   0          27m
deep-sdnc-dgbuilder-d99f9db88-skjj5         1/1       Running   0          27m
deep-sdnc-dmaap-listener-766c4747cf-rqxlh   1/1       Running   0          27m
deep-sdnc-portal-7b8c8d994d-966sm           0/1       Running   1          1m
deep-sdnc-ueb-listener-6fbd96b99d-n2wnc     1/1       Running   0          27m

with these values:
sdnc:
  global:
    persistence:
      mountPath: /onapdata
  enabled: true
  mysql:
    persistence:
      mountPath: /onapdata
    disableNfsProvisioner: true
Change has been successfully merged by Mandeep Khinda

Patch Set 1:

When testing with 3 controllers and 2 databases (no nfs-provisioner) there is a problem with the 2nd database not coming up:
deep-sdnc-0                                 0/2       Init:0/1           0          8m
deep-sdnc-1                                 0/2       Init:0/1           0          8m
deep-sdnc-2                                 0/2       Init:0/1           0          8m
deep-sdnc-db-0                              2/2       Running            0          8m
deep-sdnc-db-1                              1/2       CrashLoopBackOff   5          7m

2018-04-13T02:39:59.925449Z 0 [ERROR] InnoDB: Unable to lock ./ibdata1 error: 11
2018-04-13T02:39:59.925479Z 0 [Note] InnoDB: Check that you do not already have another mysqld process using the same InnoDB data or log files.

Patch Set 1:

> When testing with 3 controllers and 2 databases (no
 > nfs-provisioner) there is a problem with the 2nd database not
 > coming up:
 > deep-sdnc-0                                 0/2       Init:0/1     
 >      0          8m
 > deep-sdnc-1                                 0/2       Init:0/1     
 >      0          8m
 > deep-sdnc-2                                 0/2       Init:0/1     
 >      0          8m
 > deep-sdnc-db-0                              2/2       Running      
 >      0          8m
 > deep-sdnc-db-1                              1/2      
 > CrashLoopBackOff   5          7m
 > 
 > 2018-04-13T02:39:59.925449Z 0 [ERROR] InnoDB: Unable to lock
 > ./ibdata1 error: 11
 > 2018-04-13T02:39:59.925479Z 0 [Note] InnoDB: Check that you do not
 > already have another mysqld process using the same InnoDB data or
 > log files.

For APPC the decision for Beijing was to stay with one instance of mysql only, therefore current support is for one PV/PVC in non-nfs-provisioner mode.
Further need to support multiple volumes for Mysql db, maybe similarly to how I implemented for postgres or redis.
